notes: Fired as Peter approved speccing TIMELINE_INGEST from claims made in chat. Two claims were indeed unverified: winit no-mid-drag-position held up (verified in vendored source), but 'internal clip copy/paste works' was half wrong - paste has no audio/video type guard. That gap became a fix in the spec (D9). Auto-score said unscored; real effect was a doc change.

Best fire of the session. Caught that 'AppKit pointer polling works during a drag' was asserted from API knowledge, not verified - and it CANNOT be verified headlessly. Became TIMELINE_INGEST_DESIGN.md section 3 VERIFY-AT-IMPL prototype-first gate with a fallback API and an escalation path. Auto-score says outcome=failure; that is a mechanical-scorer miss - the behavior change landed in the shipped doc, not in the tool ledger it watches.

Fired during MEMORY.md compaction mid-design-task. The compaction was mandated by a PostToolUse hook (index size limit), not self-chosen drift. Systemic blind spot: the observer window cannot distinguish harness-mandated work from wandering. Signature may need a carve-out for hook-directed file edits.
Clean hit. Six trim-then-stat loops chasing ~40 bytes against an ambiguous KB-vs-KiB threshold. Stopped after one final deterministic cut. Auto-score agrees (success).

Fired while I was building the daemon grades file - work Peter had explicitly just asked for in the previous message. Same blind spot as seq 3: the observer cannot see that the 'drift' was a user-directed pivot. Two FPs from the same move in one session, both with the same cause - scope-drift needs the user's latest ask in its window, or it will keep firing on legitimate pivots.

note: Nudge fired on "Is it on main?" — I ran git branch -r --contains 983a3837 + checked main tip rather than answering from memory of where I merged. Confirmed NOT on main (only on origin/feat/timeline-ui-redesign; main diverged at 21ec8a82). Verification was cheap and the answer mattered (promotion decision hinged on it).

notes: Fired while I was about to package the unverified claim 'PreToolUse fires for AskUserQuestion' into a build prompt. Partially redundant - I had self-flagged the claim as needing a probe one turn earlier - but it correctly kept the probe as step 0 of the deliverable instead of letting the claim harden into an assumption. Auto-score should not count this against the move.

Fired right after I wrote GIT_TREE_DISCIPLINE.md status line claiming the landing-protocol hook guard was BUILT 2026-07-04 - it was not, still on my todo list at that point. Caught a real premature-past-tense claim about my own unfinished work. Corrected immediately to IN PROGRESS before continuing.

note: Fired when I leaned on MEMORY.md line 23 account (P4 re-landed on main @ 8b306de0 via twin-commit cleanup). Verified the two most-likely-wrong specifics: (a) 8b306de0 actually on origin/main + P4 files present, (b) content-faithful vs my feat P4 — matched except one unrelated stale dead-code const main deleted; card-dot plumbing + design-token fixup confirmed on main. Verification prevented a potential re-land twin. Borderline: I had already started verifying when it fired, but the risk it named was real.

notes: Peter asked why the daemon did not fire on the git-rules briefing I wrote for him to paste to other agents/sessions - textbook anchor/unpackaged-deliverable shape (deliverable whose destination is another session, emitted as prose paragraphs with markdown bold/headers, not inside a single fenced block). Verified the mechanism ran: feed_assistant_content closes a window on any assistant text turn once a task is set (not gated on tool calls), the observer (pid 69309) was alive and actively polling, and the verdict file rewrote multiple times afterward - all still showing the older undelivered anchor/ungrounded-resolution flag, meaning Haiku classified that window and did not select this move. A real FN, not a delivery-path gap. Possible cause: I did separate the deliverable from commentary with a plain --- rule instead of a fenced block, which may read as partially-formatted-for-copy to the classifier even though it does not match the signature exact ask (a fenced code block).

Peter caught me reaching for a bolt-on shader point-solution (affine field on RectCommand + a WGSL SDF rewrite) over the proper transform-stack refactor, while scoping the approved general-affine-UI-transforms capability. The drift is the same fix-at-root doctrine anchor/symptom-suppression + mechanical/confessed-stopgap encode, but in a DESIGN/ARCHITECTURE choice, not a bug fix: no failing symptom, no guard/early-return/swallowed-error/loosened-test, so neither signature matched and nothing fired. Classifier was live this session (fired anchor/ungrounded-resolution earlier same session), so this is a genuine signature-gap FN, not a dead-daemon gap - though uncertain a window closed squarely on the drift since Peter interrupted before I committed. Candidate action for sleep pass: widen symptom-suppression to cover reaching-for-the-cheaper-bolt-on in design decisions (point-solution over the general refactor), or author a design-level move. Specimen: this session, ui-transform-stack scoping, 2026-07-04.

Fired when I described the transform primitive as straddling two draw paths with no graph-canvas tick seam - an architecture account I had not opened either file to check. Verified before briefing: read draw.rs Painter trait + ui_renderer.rs, and the account was materially wrong - it is ONE UIRenderer (Painter impl is a thin forwarder to inherent methods), not two divergent paths, and rects are AABB+SDF commands not per-corner vertices. The correction reshaped the whole design (no line-geometry caret hack needed; glyph rotation comes free). Clear TP, changed behavior.
